parsed.providerName: LaGuardia Community College - Adult & Continuing Education

checklistAnswers.question: Is the Transition program currently approved in the budget?
Has the individual already completed their educational program?
Does the program have published fees?
Is the program a noncredit-bearing transition program?
Does the coursework address the individual's valued outcome?
Does the coursework address skill-building and employment outcomes?
Are all staff/volunteers/trainers screened for criminal background & excluded-provider status? (letter)
Is the program provided in an OPWDD-certified location?
Can the program be funded by ACCES-VR, IDEA, or any other source?

parsed.justification: Baruch will build vocational and workplace-readiness skills toward competitive employment.
parsed.valuedOutcome: Baruch will build vocational and workplace-readiness skills toward competitive employment.

category.label: Transition Program
capNotes: Fee sanity-check: <= $350 per course / <= $800 per month.

webChecks.label: Published fees exist
webChecks.criteria: Concrete public pricing for the program/courses must be visible.

webChecks.label: Fees are within caps ($350/course, $800/month)
webChecks.criteria: Compare stated and published fees against the caps: $350 per course / $800 per month.

webChecks.label: Program is noncredit-bearing and oriented to skill-building / employment outcomes
webChecks.criteria: Look for 'non-credit', 'continuing education', 'workforce', 'job readiness', 'career skills' framing. Credit-bearing degree programs fail.

webChecks.label: Program is not in an OPWDD-certified location
webChecks.criteria: Check the venue: a community college or ordinary community venue is fine. If the program is delivered at an OPWDD-certified/provider-agency site, it fails. If the website does not say, mark Needs Review - do not guess.

webChecks.label: Staff background-screening (needs document, not website)
webChecks.criteria: This is proven by a letter from the provider, not the website. Always mark Needs Review with the note 'needs document: background-screening letter'.

webChecks.label: Published fee matches the fee stated on the form
webChecks.criteria: Compare the fee on the application to the published fee. Verdict: matches application exactly / differs / not published.

findings.note: No concrete public pricing for courses or programs could be found on the website. Attempts to access the catalog, where fees would typically be listed, resulted in an "Access Denied" error.
findings.label: Published fees exist

findings.note: No published fees were found on the website to compare against the caps. The stated fee of $300 per course is within the $350 per course cap, but without published fees, a full comparison cannot be made.
findings.label: Fees are within caps ($350/course, $800/month)

findings.note: The website explicitly refers to "Continuing Education", "Career Skills & Workforce Training", and "COLLEGE COURSES FOR NON-CREDIT", indicating a non-credit, skill-building focus.
findings.label: Program is noncredit-bearing and oriented to skill-building / employment outcomes

findings.note: The program is offered at LaGuardia Community College, which is an ordinary community venue and not an OPWDD-certified location.
findings.quote: CUNY LaGuardia Community College 31-10 Thomson Avenue Long Island City, NY 11101 USA
findings.label: Program is not in an OPWDD-certified location

findings.note: No published fee was found on the website to compare with the stated fee of $300 per course.
findings.label: Published fee matches the fee stated on the form

rateComparison.note: No specific course fees or program costs were found on the website. Attempts to access the catalog resulted in an 'Access Denied' error.

LaGuardia Foundation ACE Scholarships

Get help with tuition and pursue your goals!
Full and partial tuition scholarships are available for eligible students.

Click Here for more information.

FIND COURSES, CERTIFICATES AND PROGRAMS
About Continuing Education

Continuing Education at LaGuardia Community College opens doors to opportunity. Whether you want to learn English, earn a high school equivalency diploma, go to college, get job training, begin or advance in a career, or grow your business, we can help you achieve your goals.

Expert Instructors
Supportive Classroom Environment
Diverse Student Body
Affordable Programs

Learn More
Read the Student Conduct Code 

Moving the Community Forward
